growth_records.cite: Chung et al. Single-cell imaging of the Mycobacterium tuberculosis cell cycle reveals linear and heterogenous growth. Nature Microbiology (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-024-01846-z. [PMC11602732]
growth_records.snippet: We observed that cell size (medians of 2.3 µm at birth and 4.5 µm at division) and growth rates (median interdivision time of 16 h) measured by live-cell imaging were consistent with those reported using bulk measurement
growth_records.notes: Growth rate calculated as mu = ln2/Td using the reported median interdivision time (Td) of 16 h from single-cell time-lapse imaging (video set A, n=363 cells) of Mtb CDC1551 SSB-GFP reporter strain in unbuffered standard 7H9 medium at 37C.

growth_records.strain: CDC1551 wild-type, FDAA (HADA) pulse-labeled (video sets B/C)
growth_records.medium: Standard supplemented Middlebrook 7H9 broth, pH-adjusted (neutral pH 7.0 or acidic pH 5.9), plus culture filtrate supplement under continuous flow
growth_records.cite: Chung et al. Single-cell imaging of the Mycobacterium tuberculosis cell cycle reveals linear and heterogenous growth. Nature Microbiology (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-024-01846-z. [PMC11602732]
growth_records.snippet: With these protocols, we were able to achieve a consistent growth pattern in Mtb over 4 days of imaging with a doubling time (~17 h) that is consistent with the doubling times of Mtb in bulk culture
growth_records.notes: Growth rate calculated as mu = ln2/Td using the approximate doubling time (~17 h) explicitly reported for the microfluidic continuous-flow protocol used for both neutral (pH 7.0, video set B) and acidic (pH 5.9, video set C) FDAA-labeled CDC1551 videos; the paper reports a single approximate value spanning both pH conditions rather than separate values, so this record represents both.

growth_records.cite: Han et al. Ribonuclease toxin RelE1 inhibits growth of Mycobacterium tuberculosis through specific cleavage of the ribosomal anti-Shine–Dalgarno region. Nucleic Acids Research (2025). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkaf1070. [PMC12620021]
growth_records.snippet: RelE1 (or RelE1 R65A) was expressed for 1 and 3 h (corresponding to one doubling time) in M. smegmatis, or 4 and 24 h (corresponding to one doubling time) in M. tuberculosis.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time of 24 h for M. tuberculosis mc2 6230 stated matter-of-factly for induction-time design, not a growth curve measured specifically in this study; medium/strain/temperature taken from this paper's Methods (7H9+10% OADC+0.05% Tween80, 37°C). growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/24 = 0.0289 h-1. Treated as a generic/reference value, hence low confidence.

growth_records.strain: not specified (generic Mtb, agent-based model parameter)
growth_records.medium: not a defined lab medium; intracellular (macrophage phagosome) and extracellular (granuloma interstitial tissue) host compartments in an agent-based simulation
growth_records.cite: Hoerter et al. Timing matters in macrophage/CD4+ T cell interactions: an agent-based model comparing Mycobacterium tuberculosis host-pathogen interactions between latently infected and naïve individuals. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01290-24. [PMC11915833]
growth_records.notes: Doubling-time range (24-48 h) explicitly given in this paper's model-parameter table for both intracellular (mtbInternalDoublingTime) and extracellular (mtbExternalDoublingTime) Mtb growth, but the values are input parameters for an agent-based simulation, sourced from cited external literature (refs 58 Repasy et al. 2013 PLoS Pathog; 75 Srivastava et al. 2020 PLoS ONE; 76 Mahamed et al. 2017 eLife) rather than measured by this study itself. No specific culture medium, temperature, or oxygen condition is given (host-tissue/in-silico context). No single point value or growth_rate_per_h can be computed since only a range is given; recorded as reported range. Confidence low because this is a borrowed/secondary parameter, not this paper's own experimental measurement. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature]

growth_records.cite: Hatfull et al. All the world's a phage. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2025). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2523344122. [PMC12582341]
growth_records.snippet: the notoriously slow growth of M. tuberculosis and some NTM strains (doubling times of 20 to 24 h) makes them challenging systems for genetic analyses, often requiring 3 to 4 wk for growth from a single cell to a bacteri
growth_records.notes: This is a narrative/background statement in an inaugural-article review (not this paper's own primary experimental data), giving only a doubling-time range (20-24 h) with no medium, strain, temperature, or culture-mode detail. Reported verbatim, not calculated. Included at low confidence because it is a directly stated numeric doubling time for a named organism, but it lacks the experimental metadata normally required to constrain a GEM; treat as a rough qualitative anchor only, not a curated data point for a specific condition.

growth_records.snippet: Briefly, 500 ml cultures were grown to steady state conditions in 1 liter chemostat fermentation vessels maintained at 37°C, with a pH of 6.9 and a generation time of 24 hours. Aerobic cultures were kept at a DOT of 10%.
growth_records.notes: Steady-state chemostat: at steady state dilution rate D = specific growth rate mu. Generation (doubling) time td = 24 h stated. mu = ln2/td = 0.693147/24 = 0.028881/h (rounded 0.0289/h); D set = mu = 0.0289/h. Chemostat feed medium composition not itemised in this paper (refers to Bacon et al. 2004), so medium_composition empty, stated false. Aeration 10% DOT, 37 C, pH 6.9 explicit. No substrate/product fluxes reported (transcriptomics paper).

growth_records.cite: Borah et al. Intracellular Mycobacterium tuberculosis Exploits Multiple Host Nitrogen Sources during Growth in Human Macrophages. Cell Reports (2019). DOI:10.1016/j.celrep.2019.11.037. [PMC6915324]
growth_records.snippet: 15N-FSRA identified 14 amino acids (minimum ratio > 0) that are available and taken up by intracellular Mtb from the macrophage ... Gln and Val are both used as nitrogen donors ... (Table 1 Flux Spectral Ranges)
growth_records.notes: Rates are FSRA flux spectral ranges = amino-acid nitrogen uptake flux expressed RELATIVE to that amino acid's nitrogen content in biomass (dimensionless), NOT absolute mmol/gDW/h. Values transcribed verbatim from Table 1 (min, max, median). FSR>1 (Gln median 2.47, Val median 2.88) = uptake exceeds biomass need => net nitrogen donor; FSR~1 (Ala) = taken up and incorporated 1:1; FSR<1 (e.g. Asn 0.13) = partly de novo synthesized. Gln identified as predominant N donor. No absolute uptake rate, mu, or doubling time is reported (48 h isotopic steady-state labeling). Serine not taken up (min FSR 0) and serC (phosphoserine transaminase) auxotroph is attenuated in macrophages. In-vitro control: Mtb grown in 7H9 broth + 5% OADC + 0.5% glycerol, and Roisin's/Rosin's minimal medium with glycerol (C) + 10 mM NH4Cl (N) + 0.1% tyloxapol, 37°C, 150 rpm, aerobic - but no numeric mu/flux reported for that condition so no separate record made.

growth_records.cite: Rustad TR et al. Mapping and manipulating the Mycobacterium tuberculosis transcriptome using a transcription factor overexpression-derived regulatory network. Genome Biology (2014). DOI:10.1186/s13059-014-0502-3. [PMC4249609]
growth_records.snippet: M. tuberculosis strain H37Rv was cultured in Middlebrook 7H9 with the ADC supplement (Difco), 0.05% Tween80 at 37C with constant agitation ... expression of a gene of interest was induced for the approximate duration of one cell doubling (1
growth_records.notes: Transcriptomics/regulatory-network paper, NOT a dedicated growth-physiology or 13C-MFA study. NO measured substrate uptake or product secretion rates are reported anywhere in the text; the only physiological rate is the wild-type H37Rv doubling time of ~18 h stated for the standard 7H9/ADC/Tween80 culture ('induced for one doubling period (approximately 18 h)'). growth_rate_per_h CALCULATED as mu=ln2/td=0.6931/18=0.0385/h. Doubling time is reported (approximate); mu is derived. Medium is a commercial Difco 7H9+ADC formulation whose full component concentrations are not stated in the paper, hence medium_composition_stated:false (only stated adjuncts listed). Induction started at OD600=0.35. The paper mapped binarized TFOE transcriptomes onto a published MTB genome-scale metabolic model (ref 33, Beste et al. GSMN-TB) to PREDICT growth rates, and measured uninduced/induced growth-rate RATIOS for 51 TFOE overexpression strains (Fig 5, Table S6) - these are engineered-strain ratios reported only as unitless uninduced/induced ratios with NO absolute mu, uptake or secretion values, so no separate per-strain records are emitted. pH not stated.

growth_records.cite: Zimmermann M et al. Integration of Metabolomics and Transcriptomics Reveals a Complex Diet of Mycobacterium tuberculosis during Early Macrophage Infection. mSystems (2017). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00057-17. [PMC5566787]
growth_records.snippet: CFUs that increased 3.6-fold between 4 and 48 h postinfection ... This corresponds to an estimated mycobacterial doubling time of 24 h, which matches our experimental data based on CFUs
growth_records.notes: Only experimental prokaryote physiology in this paper is an intracellular growth rate. Reported: CFU increased 3.6-fold between 4 and 48 h p.i. (elapsed t=44 h). Calculated doubling time td = 44 h / log2(3.6) = 44 / 1.848 = 23.8 h approx 24 h (matches the paper's stated ~24 h estimate). mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/23.8 = 0.0291 /h. No defined medium (intracellular macrophage environment); medium_composition not stated. NO experimental uptake or secretion rates are reported: all substrate-uptake/utilization values (coutilization of up to 33 nutrients) are in silico predictions from flux balance analysis on a combined genome-scale macrophage+M. tuberculosis model constrained by dual RNA-seq, hence NOT experimental and deliberately omitted from uptake_rates/secretion_rates. Temperature 37 C assumed from standard THP-1 macrophage infection culture; pH not stated.

growth_records.cite: Peterson, Eliza J.R. et al. Network analysis identifies Rv0324 and Rv0880 as regulators of bedaquiline tolerance in Mycobacterium tuberculosis. Nature Microbiology (2016). DOI:10.1038/nmicrobiol.2016.78. [PMC5010021]
growth_records.snippet: the gene overexpression was induced concurrently with drug treatment for the approximate duration of one cell doubling (18 h)... MTB strains were cultured in Middlebrook 7H9 with the ADC supplement (Difco), 0.05% Tween80 at 37 C under aerob
growth_records.notes: Approximate doubling time td = 18 h stated for wild-type MTB H37Rv in Middlebrook 7H9/ADC/0.05% Tween80, 37 C, aerobic. Growth rate computed: mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/18 = 0.0385 h-1. The 18 h is described as 'approximate duration of one cell doubling', so treat as an order-of-magnitude physiological value, not a precisely measured mu. This is a systems-biology/drug-tolerance paper (EGRIN + iMAT-PROM genome-scale model, drug time-kill assays); the genome-scale metabolic model and any fluxes are computational only, so no experimental uptake/secretion/MFA fluxes are reported. Middlebrook 7H9 and ADC are commercial formulations whose full component composition is not stated in the paper (medium_composition_stated=false). No pH given. Growth-rate reductions under bedaquiline are reported only qualitatively (Fig 2b), not as numeric mu values, so drug-treated conditions were not curated as separate rate records.
